ogTitle: Kennedys Creek vs Kennington: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Kennedys Creek and Kennington.
heading: Kennedys Creek vs Kennington — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Kennedys Creek, Victoria and Kennington, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Kennedys Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Kennington (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Kennedys Creek or Kennington safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Kennedys Creek has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Kennington scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Kennedys Creek scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Kennington scores 43/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Kennedys Creek or Kennington?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Kennedys Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
